title: Assertio accepts new buyout bid; AI biotech Isomorphic banks $2B
summary: Assertio’s latest offer represents a roughly 31% premium over an initial acquisition proposal. Elsewhere, Kyverna is readying a first-of-its-kind approval application and AC Immune is swapping CEOs.

title: New subpoena suggests DOJ has begun criminal investigation of gender-affirming care
summary: Several hospitals have received federal grand jury subpoenas regarding gender-affirming care for minors, suggesting a criminal investigation has begun.

title: STAT+: Gene therapy viruses linked to a boy’s tumor
summary: The case for Makary as the WOAT, Cassidy's seat at risk ahead of midterms, and other biotech news from The Readout

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about FDA’s Makary resigning, a Lilly weight loss drug trial, and more
summary: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary is resigning, and Kyle Diamantas, the agency's top food regulator, will step in as acting commissioner

title: STAT+: AI medical devices’ dirty FDA secret
summary: In this edition of STAT's AI Prognosis newsletter: A look at the quirks of the most common FDA medical device approval pathway, sepsis algorithms, and more.

title: Extreme heat is worsening faster for Black Americans
summary: Bill Cassidy's can't-win conundrum, what RFK Jr. gets right and wrong about antidepressants, and more health news

title: [Podcast] Closing the Distance in Cancer Care
summary: This podcast episode explores the critical role community oncology plays in the U.S. healthcare system and why supporting its growth and autonomy matters for patients and providers.

title: STAT+: Scientists link boy’s tumor to gene therapy viruses, in rare finding
summary: Doctors have linked a boy's tumor to a gene therapy, but say the rare risks need to be weighed against the profound benefits such medicines can offer.

title: STAT+: Drug meant to make gene therapy safer may also make it less effective
summary: Encoded Therapeutics gives a heads-up to other gene therapy company about the trade-offs with a drug meant to improve safety.

title: Opinion: RFK Jr.’s antidepressant deprescribing push gets one thing right — and others dangerously wrong
summary: “Deprescribing requires gradual tapering, psychological support, careful risk stratification, and accessible alternatives — none of which Kennedy’s initiative provides at scale.”

title: Opinion: What can ‘blue zones’ really teach us about aging?
summary: Have you heard about blue zones where people tend to live much longer? They’re more complicated than they might sound.

title: Opinion: Access to air conditioning is critical preventive care. But federal policy treats it as optional
summary: Access to air conditioning is critical preventive care. But federal policy treats it as optional, write Charles E. Leonard and Anthony Nicome.

title: STAT+: Bill Cassidy risked his legacy. It may not be enough to save his career
summary: Sen. Bill Cassidy risked his legacy. It may not be enough to save his career.

title: STAT+: A new kind of liver crisis is emerging in the U.S. The American diet is to blame
summary: Young people and women are particularly vulnerable to MetALD, a dangerous merging of metabolic disease and alcohol use.

title: STAT+: Why Marty Makary was the worst FDA commissioner in 25 years
summary: Marty Makary, who resigned under pressure from the FDA, brought to the job a fundamental lack of understanding of the nature of the role, STAT's Matthew Herper writes.

title: Males who discuss suicide seek help less often than females, report finds
summary: Out of 1.5 million messages received in 2025, the Crisis Text Line found that less then 20% of the texters identified as male only

title: FDA chief Marty Makary resigns from agency, ending tumultuous tenure
summary: Makary’s exit ends a turbulent run marred by leadership upheaval, mass layoffs, political pressure and public spats with drugmakers.

title: STAT+: European Union inks a draft deal to boost local production of medicines and avoid shortages
summary: The E.U. reached a provisional deal to strengthen the supply of essential medicines and avoid shortages by ‌boosting domestic production and reducing reliance on imports.

title: STAT+: Makary departs FDA amid turmoil as Diamantas, agency’s top food official, steps in
summary: Food and Drug Administration Commissioner Marty Makary is resigning from his role after a tumultuous tenure.

title: Supreme Court extends mifepristone deadline
summary: The deadliest drug in the country, the new name for PCOS, and more health news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Hims takes a $33 million hit from GLP-1 ‘pivot’
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: Hims earnings, Omada's PBM strategy, and the uphill battle faced by new sepsis algorithms.

title: STAT+: Hims’ sales miss as telehealth competition grows
summary: Bristol's partnership with Hengrui Pharma, Viiv loses HIV treatment case, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: Arvinas, Pfizer find new partner for ‘Protac’ breast cancer drug
summary: A deal with Rigel Pharmaceuticals ends months of uncertainty about the future of Veppanu, a first-of-its-kind, newly approved medicine with a murky financial outlook.

title: STAT+: STAT examines America’s deadliest drug
summary: STAT examines America's failure to address excessive alcohol use across decades.

title: FDA Finalizes Food Chemical Safety Post-Market Assessment Program, Launches Reassessment of BHT, ADA
summary: FDA continues to implement its bold food agenda, with two decisive actions focused on food chemical safety.

title: Bristol Myers, Hengrui join forces in drugmaking alliance worth up to $15.2 billion
summary: The wide-ranging pact involves as many as 13 drugs and marks the latest example of the growing relationship between Chinese pharmaceutical firms and their U.S. and Europe-based counterparts.

title: A year after Vertex’s big launch, pain drug research faces a pivotal moment
summary: Journavx revived an area of development long considered a graveyard. Can any other pain drugs keep investor excitement going?

title: GSK cuts deal with Sino to broaden reach of hepatitis B drug
summary: The marketing deal is the latest in a series of recent pacts between GSK and China-based drug companies, and could speed access to a therapy called bepirovirsen that’s important to its future.

title: Inhibrx delivers ‘differentiated’ cancer drug data; Fractyl starts diabetes gene therapy trial
summary: Inhibrx, the subject of recent buyout rumors, showed its drug might enhance the effects of Keytruda. Elsewhere, Capricor sued its cell therapy partner and Argenx got a revenue boost.

title: FDA Advances Drug Repurposing to Address Unmet Medical Needs
summary: FDA announced it is soliciting input on efforts with respect to drug repurposing to help address unmet medical needs across a range of diseases and conditions.

title: Biotech layoffs are easing, but is the worst over?
summary: BioNTech is one of the most recent companies to announce cuts, but the pace of job losses is slowing.

title: The capacity crunch in biopharma is a location problem
summary: Cell and gene therapies aren’t limited by ideas—they’re limited by where they’re built. See how “ready-now” locations change the math on scaling.

title: FDA Grants Seventh Approval under the National Priority Voucher Pilot Program
summary: FDA issued an approval for Bizengri (zenocutuzumab-zbco), a drug that treats NRG1 fusion-positive cholangiocarcinoma, an ultra-rare, aggressive cancer that forms in the bile ducts.

title: FDA Issues Guidance to Improve Collection of Pregnancy Safety Data for Drugs and Biologics
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a final guidance for industry, Postapproval Pregnancy Safety Studies, with recommendations on different methodologies that can be used in the…

title: FDA Expands AI Capabilities and Completes Data Platform Consolidation
summary: FDA announced major steps in its bold initiative to modernize the agency. The agency launched Elsa 4.0, a significant upgrade to the agency’s internal AI tool available to all FDA staff, from…
